mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a robust labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong labor market generally correlates with economic growth
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth is positive but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observations over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 5.0% signals a tight labor market, potentially leading to wage inflation and increased Fed hawkishness, which can negatively impact corporate profits and S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Low unemployment can signal economic overheating
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of periods when UNRATE is below 5.0% over a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales combined with robust industrial production indicates broad-based economic strength from both consumer demand and manufacturing output, leading to higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM growth > 0.5% AND INDPRO MoM growth > 0.3%
evidence_refs: fact:Strong consumer and industrial activity supports equity markets
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 60% of instances where both RSAFS and INDPRO growth conditions are met over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate during a monetary tightening cycle raises borrowing costs for corporations and consumers, compressing profit margins and reducing economic activity, which leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Rising interest rates typically weigh on equity valuations
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of periods following a 0.25pp rise in FEDFUNDS during a tightening cycle over a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: High year-over-year CPI inflation erodes purchasing power and can trigger aggressive monetary policy responses, increasing uncertainty and discount rates, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:High inflation can lead to market instability
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when CPIAUCSL YoY growth exceeds 3.0% over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A high level of housing starts indicates strong construction activity and consumer confidence in the housing market, which typically translates into broader economic growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Robust housing market often precedes economic expansion
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of periods when HOUST is above 1800 over a 7-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of slowing GDP growth and rising unemployment signals a deteriorating economic environment, leading to reduced corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ growth < 1.0% AND UNRATE rises > 0.5pp over 6 months
evidence_refs: fact:Economic slowdowns and rising unemployment typically harm equity markets
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where both GDP growth and unemployment conditions are met over a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production reflects healthy manufacturing and supply-side activity, indicating strong underlying economic momentum that supports corporate profitability and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a key indicator of economic health
failure_condition: If INDPRO growth is positive but S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observations over a 5-year rolling window.
